channel.title: Home on Chris Kersov
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Chris Kersov

item.title: Another Note on A blogdown Tutorial
item.description: I just discovered an awesome tutorial on blogdown written by Alison. I have to admit this is the best blogdown tutorial I have seen so far.

item.title: A Quick Note on Two Beautiful Websites
item.description:
To me, the two most impressive websites based on blogdown are:

  1. Rob J Hyndman’s personal website.
  2. Live Free or Dichotomize by Lucy and Nick et al.

I’m sure there will be more.

item.title: A Plain Markdown Post
item.description:
This sample post is mainly for blogdown users. If you do not use blogdown, you can skip the first section.

1. Markdown or R Markdown

This is a post written in plain Markdown (*.md) instead of R Markdown (*.Rmd). The major differences are:

  1. You cannot run any R code in a plain Markdown document, whereas in an R Markdown document, you can embed R code chunks (```{r});
  2. A plain Markdown post is rendered through Goldmark by default, and an R Markdown document is compiled by rmarkdown and Pandoc.

There are differences in syntax between Goldmark’s Markdown and Pandoc’s Markdown. For example, Goldmark does not support LaTeX math and Pandoc does. I have added the KaTeX support to this theme (i.e., hugo-xmin)

item.title: Lorem Ipsum
item.description:
Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut aliquip ex ea commodo consequat. Quisque mattis volutpat lorem vitae feugiat. Praesent porta est quis porta imperdiet. Aenean porta, mi non cursus volutpat, mi est mollis libero, id suscipit orci urna a augue. In fringilla euismod lacus, vitae tristique massa ultricies vitae. Mauris accumsan ligula tristique, viverra nulla sed, porta sapien. Vestibulum facilisis nec nisl blandit convallis. Maecenas venenatis porta malesuada. Ut ac erat tortor. Orci varius natoque penatibus et magnis dis parturient montes, nascetur ridiculus mus. Nulla sodales quam sit amet tincidunt egestas. In et turpis at orci vestibulum ullamcorper. Aliquam sed ante libero. Sed hendrerit arcu lacus.

item.title: CV
Education
item.description:
Education


I am currently studying Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence at the University of Bath and am on track to graduate with First-Class Honours. Previously, I achieved A*AA at A-level, studying Computer Science, Mathematics, and Further Mathematics at St Dominic's Sixth Form College.

University of Bath

BSc(Hons) Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence — First (72.42%)

Year 3 Year 2 — 72.42% Year 1 — 75.50%
Placement Year
▸▾ Modules
CM22007 - Software Engineering

CM22008 - Algorithms and Complexity

CM22009 - Machine Learning

CM22010 - Visual Computing

CM22011 - Human-Computer Interaction 1

CM22015 - Artifical Intelligence 2

CM22016 - Foundations and Frontiers of Machine Learning
▸▾ Modules
CM12001 - Artifical Intelligence 1

CM12002 - Computer Systems Architectures

CM12003 - Programming 1

CM12004 - Discrete Mathematics and Databases

CM12005 - Programming 2

CM12006 - Mathematics for Computation

St Dominic’s Sixth Form College

A-Levels — A*AA

Computer Science — A* Mathematics — A Further Mathematics — A
▸▾ Modules
Computer Systems

Algorithms and Programming
▸▾ Modules
Pure Mathematics

Statistics & Mechanics
▸▾ Modules
Core Pure 1

Core Pure 2

Further Statistics 1

Further Statistics 2

item.title: Education
item.description:
Education


I am currently studying Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence at the University of Bath and am on track to graduate with First-Class Honours. Previously, I achieved A*AA at A-level, studying Computer Science, Mathematics, and Further Mathematics at St Dominic's Sixth Form College.

University of Bath

BSc(Hons) Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence - First (72.42%)

Year 3 - Placement Year

  • On placement year at Shell in central London. I am a data scientist working within the E-Mobility team.

Year 2 - 72.42%

item.title: Experience
item.description:
Experience


My experience spans both industry and customer-facing environments, giving me a well-rounded foundation in technical problem-solving and interpersonal skills. During my placement year at Shell, I worked as a Data Scientist within the E-Mobility team, applying machine learning, data analysis, and software engineering to real-world energy and transport challenges. Prior to that, my time at Screwfix developed my communication, teamwork, and ability to perform under pressure - skills that continue to complement my technical work.


Role Company Location Timeframe
Data Scientist Shell London 08/2025 - 09/2026
Retail Assistant Screwfix Gerrards Cross, Hayes, Bath 08/2021 - 05/2025

Data Scientist —– Shell

08/2025 - 09/2026 —– London

My placement year as a Data Scientist within Shell's E-Mobility team in central London was an incredibly varied and rewarding experience, spanning data analysis, machine learning, and software engineering to support both research and development and strategic business decisions.

item.title: Interests
item.description:
Interests


This page is a snapshot of who I am outside of work and computer science - the sports I play, the places I have travelled, the things I am learning, and the hobbies I have that bring me so much joy in life.


Sports

Tennis
I have played tennis since I was 5 years old. I have always loved playing tennis. I competed regularly as a junior and have played first team for David Lloyd Northwood, Eastcote Lawn Tennis Club, and Lowlands Lawn Tennis Club. Over the past few years, I have battled through wrist and back injuries. I am still incredibly driven and motivated to keep improving in tennis, I just love it so much. I use a Head Extreme Pro with Head Lynx Tour at 54 / 52 lbs. I also have a stringing machine and string rackets for clients as a mini business.
Table Tennis
I love playing table tennis with my colleagues during lunch at work. It's a great way to take a break, reset my mind, and come back focused. Table tennis is very fast paced and requires super fast reaction times, which is one of the reasons I enjoy it so much. I quite enjoy watching table tennis as well, my favourite player is Truls Moregard.
Image placeholder: Table tennis 1
Image placeholder: Table tennis 2
Image placeholder: Table tennis 3
Badminton
I really enjoy playing badminton because it offers a unique dynamic compared to both tennis and table tennis. The game is incredibly high-intensity with explosive bursts, extreme speed, and technical skill involved. Playing doubles with my friends is so much fun and provides an excellent workout.
Image placeholder: Badminton 1
Image placeholder: Badminton 2
Image placeholder: Badminton 3
Travel and Photography

Hong Kong
I've been to Hong Kong once and it was my first ever trip to Asia. It was the greatest trip of my life.
Macau
During my first trip to Hong Kong I also went to Macau, exploring both the Macau Peninsula and Cotai.
Spain
I've been to Spain once; I went to Barcelona and Blanes. I was completely mesmerised by La Sagrada Familia.
Lithuania
Lithuania is very tranquil. I enjoy spending time in nature, especially in its forests and by its lakes.
Egypt
I stayed in Sharm El Sheikh on my trip to Egypt, where I relaxed on the beach and tried activities like diving on coral reefs and quad‑biking in the desert.
Upcoming
China
I will be visiting China and I'm excited to explore its rich culture, natural beauty, and delicious food.
Image placeholder: China 1
Image placeholder: China 2
Image placeholder: China 3
Japan
I will be visiting Japan soon. I've always wanted to go to Japan so this will be a dream come true.
Image placeholder: Japan 1
Image placeholder: Japan 2
Image placeholder: Japan 3
Food

I love food so much. I enjoy trying new dishes in different countries and cultures - I get so much enjoyment from eating. I have so many images of food I've tried that I can't choose just a few to put here. I also love cooking and baking.
Tech and Life

Tech
Laptop: M4 Macbook Air
Mouse: Logitech MX Master 3
Keyboard: Custom built keyboard
Keyboard: Logitech MX Keys
Notetaking: iPad Air 4th Gen
Headphones: Sony WH-1000XM5
Gaming: Nintendo 3DS

Image placeholder: Tech 1
Image placeholder: Tech 2
Image placeholder: Tech 3
Image placeholder: Tech 4
Image placeholder: Tech 5
Image placeholder: Tech 6
Image placeholder: Tech 7
Image placeholder: Tech 8
Rubik's Cubes
I enjoy solving Rubik's Cubes of different shapes and sizes, including the 2x2, 3x3, 4x4, 5x5, Pyraminx, and Megaminx. It is a fun mix of pattern recognition, memory, and problem-solving.
Image placeholder: Rubik's Cube 1
Image placeholder: Rubik's Cube 2
Image placeholder: Rubik's Cube 3
Personal Finances
I'm interested in staying on top of my finances. I've created an Excel spreadsheet that tracks my monthly expenses, income, and savings. It helps me visualise spending patterns and maintain awareness of my financial health. Beyond tracking, I'm interested in investing and algorithmic trading strategies. I love exploring how data science and quantitative methods can unlock better financial decision-making.
Project Ideas

Tennis Grand Slam Predictors
Football-Style Live Match Predictions
F1 Ideas
Weather Analysis
An MPO-to-Image Package
A Typing Test
A Chat Context Mover
Improving My GitHub Profile
Exploring Marketing for Projects
Rebuilding My CV in LaTeX or Typst
Quant-Related Projects
Philosophy
My everyday actions are what make me who I am. Results are just the side effects of what I do. You do it right. And do it every day. Doing stuff right just feels good. Repetition. Consistency. Care.

item.title: Projects
item.description:
Projects

This page showcases the personal projects I’ve built in my spare time. I always have so many ideas and I love being able to bring them to life. I’m always thinking about what to build next, or how to make something I’ve already built better.


Contents
  • 3DS MPO Wobble Tool
  • MPO Parser NPM Package
  • Personal Finances AI
  • Australian Open Predictor
  • Roland Garros Predictor
  • LLM Typing Test
Github Contribution Graph

3DS MPO Wobble Tool

Link —– GitHub

Streamlit Pillow NumPy Streamlit Community Cloud
A web tool that converts Nintendo 3DS .mpo stereo image files into animated wobble GIFs. The 3DS captured true stereoscopic photos - two slightly offset images stored in a single file - but the format is almost universally unsupported outside the handheld itself. This tool extracts the stereo pair, aligns the frames using a pixel-difference score, and encodes them into a smoothly crossfaded, looping GIF that conveys the original depth and parallax allowing it to be viewable on any device with no special hardware required.
7 photos
View Gallery
README.md
▸▾ Toggle preview
Loading README...


Personal Finances AI

GitHub

Streamlit openpyxl Ollama qwen2.5:7b
A Streamlit app that reads a personal Excel finance workbook — one sheet per month, tracking needs, wants, savings, income, and spending by category — and uses a locally running LLM to generate a plain-English summary of the current month. It produces a monthly digest covering cash flow, budget goal tracking, top spending categories, and a forward-looking observation. Everything runs fully offline on the machine; the Excel file is never committed to the repo.
1 photo
View Gallery
README.md
▸▾ Toggle preview
Loading README...
